headline: Péter Magyar, the candidate aiming to unseat Orban as Hungary's leader
subhead: Previously a member of the Fidesz party, the 45-year-old former senior civil servant slammed the door on those in power in 2024 when he condemned corruption in the prime minister's inner circle. Surrounding himself with legislative election candidates who owe him everything, he has promised to ease relations with the EU.

Paris mayor Emmanuel Grégoire seeks to 'unblock' talks over sale of Parc des Princes to PSG
subhead: The new mayor of Paris has called a Council meeting for April 14, during which he will ask the city's 163 elected officials to authorize the start of negotiations with the football club over the stadium's future.

headline: US consumer inflation surges 3.3% year-on-year amid impact of Iran war
subhead: Gasoline prices surged by 21.2% between February and March – the largest monthly increase since the the government began publishing a gasoline price index in 1967.

headline: Céline Dion fans in Paris face  'Hunger Games' ticket scramble
subhead: The international star has chosen Paris for her big comeback this fall. Overwhelming demand combined with the complexity of securing tickets has set off a kind of royal battle on social media, complete with tutorials.

headline: Thomas Piketty: '1789, 1945, 2026: Three French debt crises'
subhead: To tackle public debt, France remains wealthy with solid underlying economic fundamentals. Yet leaders must confront prevailing 'political conservatism' and the wealthiest's behavior, argues economist Thomas Piketty.

subhead: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

headline: A French judge was found burned at a cliff in Djibouti in 1995. How and why he died remains a mystery
subhead: On October 19, 1995, the body of Bernard Borrel was found burned at the base of a cliff in Djibouti. More than 30 years later, his widow Elisabeth Borrel continues her fight to shed light on the case, which has become entangled in military interests, diplomatic pressure and state secrets.

headline: Israel pounds Lebanon and worries ceasefire with Iran could drag into endless negotiations
subhead: The Israeli military launched its largest offensive against Hezbollah on Wednesday. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu fears Donald Trump could pressure him into accepting a rushed agreement with Iran.
